content.full_text:
Fa,rn Porrucrl Pnecrrcns CouvrrssroN
P.O. Box 807 . .128 J Street . Sacranrento. CA f-isll-0807
(916) 322-5660 . Fax (916) .lll-()tltt6
October 18, 2000
Kenneth J. Yood
Paul, Hastings, Janofsky & Walker
555 South Flower Street
Los Angeles, CA 90071-2371
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.I-00-220
Dear Mr. Yood:
This letter is in response to your letter dated September 21,2000, regarding "Lindy
Batara's Challenge to the Candidacy of William F. Nicholson, M.D. for a seat on the
Washington Township Healthcare District Board of Directors." You represent the Washington
Township Healthcare District. Since your request relates to past conduct, we decline to provide
formal written advice. (Regulation 18329(bX8XA).) Instead, we are treating your request as one
for informal assistance under the Political Reform Act (the "Act').I Informal assistance does not
confer the immunity provided by formal written advice. (Regulation 1s329(c)(3).)
Moreover, please note that the Commission's jurisdiction is limited to the Political
Reform Act. The other provisions of law to which you refer, such as district law and the
common law doctrine of incompatible offices, are outside the Act. The Commission has no
jurisdiction to interpret or enforce these laws.
DISCUSSION
The Act requires the following:
a Every public official must disclose all of his or her economic interests that could
foreseeably be affected by the.exercise of the official's duties. (Section 81002(c),
87200-873t3.)
' Government Code sections 81000 - 91015. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109 - 18996, of
the Califomia Code of Regulations.


File No. I-00-220
Page2
. Every agency must adopt a conflict of interest code. (Section 87300.) Article 7,
Chapter 3 sets out specific requirements concerning the procedures for adoption and
promulgation of the codes, as well as substantive criteria for the provisions of each
code.
I Section 87302(a) provides that a conflict of interest code must contain a specific
enumeration of the positions within the agency that involve the making or
participation in the making of decisions which may foreseeably have a material
financial effect on any financial interest.
. With respect to each such position, a code is required to list the specific types of
investments, interests in real property and income that must be disclosed. Section
873020) provides that a conflict of interest code must require each designated
employee to file statements of economic interests (Form 700) that disclose reportable
investments, business positions, interests in real property and income. Thus, the
financial information that must be disclosed on a designated employee's statement of
economic interests is prescribed by the agency's conflict of interest code.
You note in your letter that the conflict of interest code of the district meets and exceeds
the requirements of the Act. The responsibility for determining if a code meets the specifications
of the Act rests with the "code reviewing body." (Section 87303.) Your code reviewing body
appears to be the Alameda County Board of Supervisors. The Commission may provide
technical assistance to agencies in the preparation of conflict of interest codes, however, each
agency is responsible for adopting a code appropriate to its individual circumstances. (Section
87312.)
However, please note that the Act prohibits a code reviewing body from approving a
conflict of interest code which includes requirements beyond those set out in section 87302.
While section 81013 of the Act permits govemment agencies to impose additional requirements
on any person as long as such requirements do not prevent the person from complying with the
Act, such obligations may not be imposed as part of the conflict of interest code mandated by the
Act. A conflict of interest code adopted pursuant to the Act may not exceed the prohibitions of
the Act. (See Alperin Opinion, 3 FPPC Ops. 77 (Aug. 8,1976, No. 76-084), copy enclosed.)
ln other words, a goveflrment agency is not prohibited from adopting resJrictions which
go beyond those contained in the Act, so long as it does so under its own authority and so long as
those requirements do not prevent a person from complying with the Act. (Section 81013; In re
Miller (1976) 2 FPPC Ops. 91.) The Act does not prevent the dishict from adopting it own rule


File No. I-00-220
Page 3
governing disqualification from govemmental decisions. However, the district may not base
those requirements on the Act or any conflict of interest code adopted pursuant to the Act.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at
(er6) 322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
Enc.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Washington Township Healthcare District's conflict of interest code, which exceeds the requirements of the Political Reform Act, complies with state law regarding disqualification and economic interest disclosure requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice on past conduct but provides informal assistance. A conflict of interest code cannot exceed the prohibitions of the Act, though agencies may adopt additional restrictions under their own authority outside the code framework, provided such restrictions do not prevent compliance with the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: Document is informal assistance correspondence rather than formal advice letter; no QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in traditional format; contains DISCUSSION section explaining PRA requirements and limitations; letter dated October 18, 2000; requestor represented Washington Township Healthcare District through Paul, Hastings law firm.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Washington Township Healthcare District's conflict of interest code, which exceeds the requirements of the Political Reform Act, complies with state law regarding disqualification and economic interest disclosure requirements.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declines to provide formal written advice on past conduct but provides informal assistance. A conflict of interest code cannot exceed the prohibitions of the Act, though agencies may adopt additional restrictions under their own authority outside the code framework, provided such restrictions do not prevent compliance with the Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal assistance letter regarding whether the Washington Township Healthcare District's conflict of interest code appropriately implements Political Reform Act disclosure requirements and disqualification rules. The FPPC clarifies that conflict of interest codes cannot exceed Act requirements, though agencies may adopt additional restrictions separately.
